frame_analyses.description: Title screen showing 'Suturing' with instructor credits from City University London
Needle driver grasping needle at appropriate position for driving
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper needle grip demonstrated at 2/3 point from tip

frame_analyses.description: Needle being driven through pink fabric material with two instruments working together
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Coordinated bimanual technique shown with counter-traction

frame_analyses.description: Needle exit through material with good perpendicular angle
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean needle exit demonstrates proper driving technique

frame_analyses.description: Beginning of knot formation with suture wrapping around instrument
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Demonstrating proper wrap technique for surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: Completing double throw for surgeon's knot with careful tension control
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double wrap clearly demonstrated for initial throw security

frame_analyses.description: Single throw being formed with opposite hand leading
Final single throw completion with instrument positioning
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Completing three-throw sequence with alternating directions

frame_analyses.description: Scissors positioned to cut suture tails close to knot
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper tail length maintained before cutting

confidence_rationale: This is an instructional video, not a performance to be scored
cannot_determine: Performance timing - this is a demonstration video
technique_summary: Comprehensive instructional demonstration of intracorporeal suturing technique including proper needle handling, suture placement, and three-throw knot sequence
strengths: Clear demonstration of surgeon's knot double throw technique
Good visualization of hand switching between throws
Proper needle driving angle and grip position shown

demonstrated_techniques: proper needle grip at 2/3 point from tip
verbal_cues: Title indicates continuation with 'one more stitch'
common_errors_addressed: demonstrates proper needle grip position to avoid slipping
shows appropriate wrap count for surgeon's knot security
illustrates correct hand alternation between throws
